title: Test to verify overlay quotas work, show container overhead on quota

comments.body: @haircommander looks like some test unhappiness here
uh turns out aws boxes don't have loop devices... Should I create them if this is the case?
@rhatdan if this doesn't work i don't think It can be included in the automated test suite, it's the only solution the internet had to offer :\
I will keep wrestling with it tomorrow, but it seems the amazon instances aren't playing nice, so this may not work in CI :'(
@haircommander If you can't get it working on CI, we should see about adding it to the baseline test script maintained by @TomSweeneyRedHat
@haircommander if you decide to move it there, the baseline tests are at: https://github.com/projectatomic/libpod/blob/master/test/test_podman_baseline.sh
@TomSweeneyRedHat @rhatdan @mheon anyone know why this keeps failing? I've repushed like 4 times and I keep getting the same error
Cloud podman tests seem to be failing everywhere right now - I'd ignore them at the moment
Right this failure is a problem with the cloud image. Caused by a bad libsemanage package.

:pushpin: Commit 0ea495e has been approved by `rhatdan`
